summary: Architecture review requests changes before SIM-001 acceptance: make validateWorldStateV0 structurally safe for malformed unknown input, add runtime table uniqueness/exact coverage invariants, and strengthen architecture/static guard coverage for DOM, Node fs/path IO, real-time APIs, and render-frame APIs.

tests_run.result: Vitest ran tests/sim-core-world-state.test.ts: 1 file passed, 7 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 2 expected violation(s).
Malformed definitions.polities containing null threw TypeError instead of returning structured invalid-schema.

decisions: Do not accept SIM-001 until malformed unknown WorldState input is handled without throwing.
Do not accept SIM-001 until runtime state tables validate duplicate and missing definition rows.
Do not accept SIM-001 until architecture/static guard coverage proves the required forbidden API categories.
risks: Without structural validation, external malformed data can bypass structured rejection and crash validation.
Without runtime table cardinality checks, authoritative state can pass semantic validation while missing or duplicating runtime rows.
Without stronger static guard coverage, SIM-001 cannot prove the architecture boundary requested by the task.
requested_action: Fix only SIM-001 scope issues: add nested structural validation and no-throw tests for malformed unknown input, add runtime state uniqueness/exact coverage invariants and tests, and strengthen architecture/static guard and fixture coverage for DOM, Node fs/path IO, real-time APIs, and render-frame APIs. Re-run SIM-001 required checks and return an updated handoff to systems_architect.
